stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates ongoing community interest and evaluation of Meta's latest open-source language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern AI/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A new malware, Crypto Clipper, has been discovered that steals cryptocurrency.
The backdoor is lightweight and spreads via USB drives.
The malware utilizes Tor for its communication, indicating advanced evasion techniques.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning language models on insecure code can cause emergent misalignment due to poorly understood internal structures.
The study proposes and tests an 'actionable activation-space direction' to detect this misalignment across multiple model families (Qwen2.5, Gemma-2, Llama-3.2, Mistral-3).
A difference-in-means direction achieved a high separation rate (99.6%) between aligned and misaligned activations at the final layer of the tested models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on modern LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is text generation.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also demonstrates improved memory efficiency when running models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI capabilities on Macs.
The feature is currently available in preview, signaling a move toward optimized, native Apple ML tooling.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging workflows directly to local AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It simplifies the setup process by eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.
The tool supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.

stories.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: The Atlantic has made four datasets of music used to train AI models publicly searchable.
Two of the identified music training datasets are massive, containing 12 million and 9 million tracks.
The story highlights the transparency and scale of data being used in AI model training.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: The FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of internal agency controversy.
A specific political interference incident involved a Trump official refusing review in February.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for national defense.
The increased defense spending is noted to potentially boost Taiwanese business overseas.
The context of China's proximity highlights the strategic military importance of this development.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses a key limitation in state-of-the-art flow-matching TTS: the inability to adapt to new pronunciation errors post-deployment.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that learns pronunciation corrections via latent conditioning edits rather than full model retraining.
The method optimizes token-level perturbations in the text embedding space when corrective feedback is provided, allowing for efficient, targeted updates.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces StylisticBias, a new benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses the critical gap in understanding how visual cues influence MLLMs' judgments of people in consequential settings.
The benchmark uses controlled photorealistic faces to separate appearance effects from inherent identity differences.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The paper proposes a novel method for LLM alignment that utilizes implicit user feedback instead of relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
Existing LLM alignment methods are limited by the high cost and scarcity of explicit preference annotations from users.
The research aims to leverage implicit signals, similar to those used by major internet platforms, to improve model alignment efficiency.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local hardware.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Polymarket reportedly paid people to post fake videos of themselves placing bets
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Polymarket, a prediction market platform, is reportedly paying individuals to create deceptive content.
The content involves filming fake bets and celebrating fabricated wins on social media.
An investigation by the Wall Street Journal uncovered over 1,100 such deceptive clips.

stories.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
stories.author: Matt Burgess, Maddy Varner, May Bulman, Gabriel Geiger, WIRED.com
bullet_points: The UK government plans to implement facial scanning technology for age verification of asylum seekers.
Independent tests have demonstrated significant risks associated with the technology, including potential for life-altering errors.
The decision to proceed with the flawed technology despite known risks highlights a conflict between policy implementation and technological reliability.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has directed Northrop Grumman to cease work on the lunar HALO module.
The decision involves the reassignment of most employees affected by the program change.
This represents a shift or cancellation within a specific NASA/aerospace contract.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in AI.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity on how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It introduces cross-attention attribution, adapting the DAAM framework to the speech domain for the first time, specifically for speech diffusion models.
The method generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers to improve the diagnosis of failure modes and controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured state representation.
Addresses a key limitation in standard agents where task states (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service where agents must maintain context across tool calls and policy adherence.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: Introduces DeepSWIP, a new framework for counterfactual reasoning in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
Addresses the limitation of standard associational inference by providing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
Achieves this by using neural materialization to map neural predicates to standard ProbLog choices and applying SWIPs.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: The paper introduces SARLO-80, a new worldwide dataset designed to bridge the gap between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data.
Existing SAR datasets are often limited by low-resolution, intensity-only products, failing to capture complex-valued SAR measurements or native acquisition geometry.
The creation of this high-quality, large-scale dataset is crucial for advancing physically grounded multimodal foundation models in remote sensing and AI.

stories.title: Sovereign Execution Brokers: Enforcing Certificate-Bound Authority in Agentic Control Planes
stories.author: Deying Yu
bullet_points: Addresses the security gap where autonomous agents' non-deterministic reasoning processes could lead to unauthorized production mutations.
Introduces the Sovereign Execution Broker (SEB) as a runtime enforcement boundary for certified authority.
Distinguishes the need for a mandatory enforcement point that goes beyond traditional identity authorization or action certification.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of LiveCodeBench (LCB) to support multiple programming languages.
LCB is an established, contamination-aware benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation tasks, currently limited to Python.
The research addresses the need for LLMs to generalize their coding capabilities across diverse programming languages.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM that operates significantly within a continuous latent space.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
The study decomposes transparency into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a fine-grained alternative to traditional Differential Privacy (DP).
Addresses the costly worst-case privacy-accuracy tradeoff inherent in standard DP mechanisms.
The framework explicitly models attacker knowledge, compromised data portions, and query types to measure leakage incrementally.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in current LLM serving systems by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device AI, unlike high-throughput KV caching methods.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' for checkpointing and restoring the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent state resets and tight responsiveness.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: Addresses the need for LLM coding agents to possess high-level operational knowledge about a codebase beyond the code itself.
Investigates the effectiveness of using context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide coding agents, where current research is inconclusive.
Proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine' tuning method to improve repository guidance for these agents.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework for maintaining structured task states in AI agents.
Addresses the limitation of standard agents where state must be reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses on enabling policy-adherent tool-calling agents, particularly relevant for customer service domains.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale bilingual (German/English) dataset of 1.2M CT/MR image-text pairs for radiology.
Proposes RefRad2D to enable scalable training of Spatially Grounded Vision-Language Models (VLMs) without manual spatial annotations.
The resulting model, RadGrounder, performs joint tasks including report generation, visual question answering, and spatial grounding.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between online hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for generating counterspeech.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating non-repetitive and relevant responses against harmful online exchanges.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-oriented inference optimization techniques crucial for scalable and low-cost large model services.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
The research provides a systematic review of key technologies and current industry status across these optimization levels.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF) for analyzing Nigerian public discourse.
MIF is a nine-dimension schema designed to differentiate true communicative intent from surface-level sentiment.
The research argues that AI failure in Nigerian discourse stems from 'context failure' rather than translation issues, criticizing existing three-way sentiment benchmarks.

stories.title: Apple Internals: Swift in the Kernel
stories.author: blog.calif.io via heavyrain266
bullet_points: The article discusses Apple's internal use of the Swift programming language within its kernel.
This suggests a significant shift or integration of Swift into low-level, core operating system components.
The topic is highly relevant to Apple's engineering practices and OS architecture.

stories.title: Shadow Deployments for AI Agents: Canary Your Prompt Changes Before They Burn Production
stories.author: saurav_bhattacharya
bullet_points: Introduces the concept of 'Shadow Deployments' specifically for AI agents.
Proposes using canary testing methodologies to validate prompt changes before full production rollout.
Addresses the real-world risk of prompt tweaks causing unexpected failures in deployed AI systems.
